markdown.markdown:
My research examines how biodiversity financing mechanisms shape the content and distribution of ecological investment. I focus on the contextual adequacy of instruments across income regimes and governance structures — from official development finance and voluntary carbon markets to sovereign debt and national budget reform. --- **Chronicle of a Debt Foretold: Green Debt Strategies in a Fossil Fuel-Dependent Economy** with Antoine Godin, Louis Daumas, Jeffrey Althouse & Romain Svartzman *International Review of Economics and Finance* 109, 2026 [[DOI]](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.iref.2026.105301)
**Selective Carbon Credits: Market Preferences and Ecosystem Restoration in Senegal** with Rémi Prudhomme, Marieme Ba, Penda Diop, Tamsir Mbaye, Harold Levrel & Adrien Comte *Ecological Economics* 235, 2025 [[DOI]](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolecon.2025.108626)
**Subsidies against Nature: A Multidimensional Framework for Biodiversity-Aligned National Budgets** with Améline Vallet, Vincent Deschamps, Amélie Le Mieux, Aurélien Oosterlinck, Hélène Soubelet, Louise Dupuis & Harold Levrel *Ecological Economics* 235, 2025 [[DOI]](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolecon.2025.108661)
**Economic Knowledge for Nature Preservation: An Epistemic Inquiry into Spatial Prioritisation and International Action** *Global Africa* 11, 2025 [[DOI]](https://doi.org/10.57832/4gcb-e751) --- ## Under Review **New Nature–Economy Models are Needed to Understand the Macroeconomic and Financial Implications of the Ecological Transition** with Romain Svartzman, Jeffrey Althouse, Katie Kedward, Adam Poupard, Bastien Bedossa, Étienne Espagne & Angela Modica Scala Submitted to *Nature Perspectives*
**Systematic Classification of Biodiversity-Related Aid Using Natural Language Processing** with Kerstin Wildgruber, Moritz Tötzke & Florian Egli Submitted to *Nature Sustainability* --- ## Ongoing Work **Who Owns the Green Transition? Unequal Access to Finance in a Dynamic Framework** *In preparation*
**Market–Policy Coordination for Environmental Objectives across Institutional Contexts** *Job market paper*
**Bioeconomy and Responsibility: An Ethical Framework for the Hybridisation of Knowledge** with Felwine Sarr — *in preparation* --- ## Working Papers & Preprints **Après l'encastrement. Savoirs économiques et transformations écologiques** with Hugo Mosneron Dupin *CIRED Working Papers* 2026-99, 2026
**Chronicle of a Debt Foretold: Sovereign Debt Management Strategies for Colombia's Climate and Biodiversity Transition** with Antoine Godin, Louis Daumas, Jeffrey Althouse & Romain Svartzman *CEPR Discussion Paper*, 2025 [[Paper]](https://cepr.org/publications/dp20443)
**Avenues for Exploring Nature Scenarios in Ecological Macroeconomic Models** with Katie Kedward *SSRN Preprint* 5200146, 2025 [[Preprint]](https://ssrn.com/abstract=5200146)
**From Climate to Nature Macro-Criticality: Exploring New Scenarios and Modelling Frameworks** with Jeffrey Althouse, Bastien Bedossa, Étienne Espagne, Léna Faucher, Katie Kedward, Angela Modica Scala & Adam Poupard *CEPR Discussion Paper*, 2025
**Building a Multidimensional Biodiversity Harmful Subsidies Indicator to Support Transition Policies: The Case of France** with Améline Vallet, Vincent Deschamps, Aurélien Oosterlinck, Amélie Le Mieux, Hélène Soubelet, Louise Dupuis & Harold Levrel *SSRN Scholarly Paper* 4713237, 2024 [[Preprint]](https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.4713237)
**Bridging the Gap in Biodiversity Financing: A Review of Assessments of Existing and Needed Financial Flows for Biodiversity** with Romain Svartzman & Jeffrey Althouse *UCL Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose*, 2024
**Financer la restauration écologique : un paysage en mutation. Le cas du Sénégal** with Pape Diop, Mamadou Kande, Quentin Merour, Rémi Prudhomme & Adrien Comte *Cahiers de l'OHMi Téssékéré*, CNRS, no. 12 (2024): 29–41 --- ## Policy Briefs & Blog Posts **Biodiversity COP 16: Where EU Member States Stand on National Biodiversity Strategies and Action Plans** with Jeffrey Althouse, Léna Faucher & Romain Svartzman *Institute for European Policymaking, Bocconi University*, 2024 [[Post]](https://iep.unibocconi.eu/biodiversity-cop-16-where-eu-member-states-stand-national-biodiversity-strategies-and-action-plans)
**Limiter l'artificialisation des sols pour éviter une dette écologique se chiffrant en dizaines de milliards d'euros** with Chloé Surun & Harold Levrel *The Conversation*, August 25, 2021 [[Article]](https://theconversation.com/limiter-lartificialisation-des-sols-pour-eviter-une-dette-ecologique-se-chiffrant-en-dizaines-de-milliards-deuros-166073)
**Zéro artificialisation nette : de la Convention citoyenne au texte de loi, une trajectoire en débat** with Clara Claron, Luc Elie & Sarah Batellier *Fonciers en débat*, June 15, 2021 [[Article]](https://fonciers-en-debat.com/zero-artificialisation-nette-de-la-convention-citoyenne-au-texte-de-loi-une-trajectoire-en-debat/)
**Stop à la neutralité carbone en trompe-l'œil : l'empreinte carbone au service d'une nouvelle stratégie industrielle** *Institut Rousseau*, December 7, 2021 [[Article]](https://institut-rousseau.fr/stop-a-la-neutralite-carbone-en-trompe-loeil-lempreinte-carbone-au-service-dune-nouvelle-strategie-industrielle/)
**Faire évoluer les outils comptables pour "réencastrer" l'activité économique dans son environnement** with B. Métadier *Institut Rousseau*, July 2, 2020 [[Article]](https://institut-rousseau.fr/faire-evoluer-les-outils-comptables-pour-reencastrer-lactivite-economique-dans-son-environnement/) --- ## Master Thesis **Une dette écologique pour fonder les politiques de préservation du vivant** Defining a national accounting framework in strong sustainability adapted to the fight against land artificialisation. Université Paris Dauphine, 2021 — *Veblen Prize (Master's Thesis Award, 2022)* [[Full text]](https://www.veblen-institute.org/IMG/pdf/19_-_m_-_une_dette_ecologique_pour_fonder_lespolitiques_de_preservation_du_vivant.pdf)
